headlines.title: Indian Oil Raises LPG and Jet Fuel Prices
headlines.summary: Indian Oil Co. has hiked the price of liquefied petroleum gas for local industrial users and the price of jet fuel, but only for foreign airlines, Reuters has reported, citing a company statement. headlines.title: UAE Break With OPEC Puts African Crude Exports At Risk
headlines.summary: A couple of days ago, the United Arab Emirates announced that it will formally leave OPEC on May 1, with the Middle East oil giant becoming the latest country to cut ties with the organization in recent years. headlines.title: $6 Gas in California Signals New Phase of the Global Energy Crunch
headlines.summary: The statewide average for 87-octane gasoline in California has topped $6 a gallon as the Iran-war-driven global energy crunch ripples across the West Coast, the hardest-hit U.S. region. headlines.title: Net Crude Supply Loss is 9 Million Bpd Despite Surge in Atlantic Exports: Vortex
headlines.summary: The Strait of Hormuz blockade has removed roughly 9 million barrels per day of crude supply from the global market, and replacement barrels are not keeping pace, according to a new report by Vortexa. headlines.title: War Puts LNG Future in the Spotlight
headlines.summary: Imports of liquefied natural gas into Asia last month slumped to the lowest in seven years. headlines.title: Trump Eyes More U.S. Oil Output as Iran War Squeezes Supply

headlines.title: UK Airlines Race to Secure Jet Fuel as Summer Travel Crisis Looms
headlines.summary: The government is scrambling to secure UK airlines’ emergency access to jet fuel as global shipments fall to their lowest level on record. headlines.title: ENEOS Takes 10% Stake in Malaysia LNG Tiga in New Deal With Petronas
headlines.summary: One of Japan’s biggest energy firms, ENEOS, is re-entering the joint venture operating the Malaysia LNG Tiga project with a 10% stake under a deal it has signed with Malaysia’s state oil and gas firm Petronas.
